items.title: Origin Energy investigating 'potential' customer data breach

children.content: Origin Energy says it does not believe customer credit card or bank details have been affected by a suspected data breach at the company.

gallery.altText: Origin's red and white logo is seen on a pile of company reports

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: Australians don't understand how interest rates work, RBA finds

children.content: The Reserve Bank is concerned that too many Australians have little idea what the central bank is trying to achieve with interest rates, which it says could be making its job harder.

image.altText: A red up arrow next to a percentage sign on top of an aerial view of dozens of houses.

items.title: 'It has tested me': Rebel Wilson responds to defamation case win

children.content: Hollywood actor Rebel Wilson successfully defends a defamation case brought against her by the star of her directorial debut.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court April 30 for defamation trial

items.title: Hanson labels domestic violence 'two-way street'

children.content: The One Nation leader said women should not 'put up with' domestic violence, as she highlighted the cost of living pressures on families.

image.altText: A man and a woman at a press conference

items.title: Why the Houthi threat against Saudi ships 'spells real trouble' for the war

children.content: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

articles.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

image.altText: Woman at airport

articles.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

gallery.altText: Three soldiers holding what appear to be large missiles/guns sitting together.

thumbnail.altText: Three soldiers holding what appear to be large missiles/guns sitting together.

items.title: Lewis breaks Australian 100m record, beats five-time Olympic champ

children.content: Torrie Lewis shows she is hitting her straps ahead of the Commonwealth Games by beating her own record mark during a meet in Slovakia.

image.altText: Torrie Lewis unties her shoelaces while sitting on the track

items.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE diagnosis

children.content: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

image.altText: Paul Wade

items.title: 25,000-year-old grass-burning ritual unearthed in Victorian cave

children.content: Evidence of ancient burnt grasses unearthed in a Victorian cave suggest the old ancestors of the GunaiKurnai people used the cave for fire rituals during the Ice Age.

gallery.altText: GunaiKurnai Elder Uncle Russell Mullett at the cave entrance of Cloggs Cave
Microscopic images of burnt grass phytoliths from Cloggs Cave.
Two men standing in a limestone cave

thumbnail.altText: GunaiKurnai Elder Uncle Russell Mullett at the cave entrance of Cloggs Cave

Two men standing in a limestone cave

items.title: 'These things didn’t happen': Senior Catholic cleric denies sex abuse

children.content: The lawyer for a former Catholic bishop who allegedly sexually abused three young men tells his trial the incidents did not happen, and warns the jury not to be affected by possible prejudice against the church.

image.altText: A mid-shot of former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Saunders, outside court wearing a dark coat, grey scarf and grey flat cap.

items.title: Voluntary assisted dying bill has numbers to pass, NT government says

children.content: The Northern Territory is set to make voluntary assisted dying legal in August. The ABC understands all 17 Country Liberal Party government members will vote in favour of the Rights of the Terminally Ill Act.

image.altText: VAD brochure

items.title: Six beluga whales make successful journey by air from Canada to US

children.content: Six beluga whales from a defunct marine park in Canada have been successfully moved to new homes in the US.

gallery.altText: A beluga whale in the air in a sling with its side flippers poking through a hole.
A white beluga whale in a sling being moved along rollers.
A smiling woman with her fingers in the mouth of a beluga whale in a sling.

items.title: The claims and evidence behind popular wellness treatments

children.content: Wellness and anti-aging clinics are popping up across Australia. But do their treatments work and are they safe?

items.title: Network 10 moves 10 News+ from weekdays

children.content: Network 10 says its 10 News+ news and current affairs program is moving from its nightly weekday slot and will be aired on Sunday nights only.

image.altText: A woman in a dark red dress stands next to a man in a shirt and suit jacket. Both are smiling.

items.title: As The Odyssey smashes box office records, one name is drawing people in

children.content: The Odyssey's director Sir Christopher Nolan has a stellar catalogue. Interstellar, even. But as his new film smashes box office records, is it safe to crown him the new king of Hollywood?

children.content: Women who have lost children talk about continuing in the wake of such grief, and how people stepped up, or stepped away.

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

items.title: Gates Foundation met with Epstein about 30 times, review finds

children.content: Bill Gates was aware of Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 sex offence conviction during their association, an external review of the Gates Foundation finds.

image.altText: Gates walks among a crowd of reports and Capitol Hill staff, giving a small, tight-lipped smile.

items.title: Jewish community group details 'unprecedented' security needs

children.content: Police engagement failed to engender a sense of safety in Melbourne's Jewish community in the face of rising "vitriol", the antisemitism royal commission has heard.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a blue blazer stands in front of a Royal Commission sign.

items.title: Boy charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister in Perth

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

image.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway

items.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

children.content: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

articles.title: Fox sisters combine in 'dream team' for gold medals at World Championships

children.content: Noemie Fox labels her partnership with older sister Jess a "dream team" after the pair combined for another set of gold medals at the IFC Canoe Slalom World Championships in Oklahoma.

items.title: Judge says there's a 'real risk' to safety of Bondi Beach attack witnesses

children.content: Twenty witnesses in the Bondi Beach terror attack case have been granted anonymity, with a judge saying there is a "real risk" to their safety.

image.altText: Flowers laid in tribute to bondi victims.

items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

image.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

items.title: Qld government denies blocking report into response to domestic violence

children.content: The report was particularly critical of police responses to domestic and family violence incidents, and highlighted a system "marked by fragmentation, inconsistency, and significant procedural and cultural barriers".

image.altText: Shannon Fentiman holds up a report while speaking to media during a press conference.

items.title: Mum loses second son in car crash as driver granted bail

children.content: A 30-year-old charged with manslaughter has been released on bail following a crash that has left a Queensland mother childless 16 years after the death of her eldest son.

image.altText: A smiling man with a dark beard.

items.title: Atlassian toasts $1b hybrid tower milestone after axing 500 jobs for AI

children.content: When construction finishes next year, the Sydney building will be the tallest hybrid structure in the world by 100 metres.

items.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Albanese's career mirrors Howard's in two important ways

children.content: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

items.title: Woolworths charged over NT crash between forklift, motorcyclist

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error

children.content: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

items.title: Son's dying wish to see mum play soccer now helps her cope with loss

children.content: After Spencer Farley was diagnosed with terminal cancer, he asked his mum to start playing soccer. In the 12 months since his death, the game has kept her going.
